customer_anxiety_profile: Low anxiety, high busyness. Ken is warm and cooperative but explicitly self-described as 'not in a massive rush' and 'in the planning stages.' His pace is deliberate, not avoidant — he has a concrete plan (HELOC, roof first, then solar) and is moving on his own timeline. The risk is not resistance; it's drift if the appointment doesn't feel urgent to him.
unresolved_concerns: Utility bill not yet received — appointment is conditional on Ken sending it tonight
Ken's stated preference to do roof first, then solar — closer needs to address whether the combined estimate appointment covers both or requires separate scheduling
Spring timeline: Ken expects to 'pull the trigger' in spring — closer should calibrate proposal framing to this horizon, not push for immediate close
Ground mount interest raised and dismissed — Ken may still have questions about system placement options the closer should be prepared to address
sensitive_topics_raised: HELOC financing in progress — Ken has a specific financing vehicle; closer should not assume cash or standard solar financing
Busy life context (college football, cross country, work) — Ken is time-constrained; the closer should honor the one-hour block and not run long
exact_promises_rep_made: 09:30 appointment slot held pending bill receipt
One hour blocked for the appointment
Appointment will include both a roof estimate and a solar estimate
closer_should_know: This is a recycled lead — Ken previously spoke with Meg and was asked to send his utility bill, which he has not yet done. The appointment is conditional on bill receipt tonight. Closer should confirm the bill arrived before the appointment and check whether Ion already has Ken's bill from Meg's prior interaction before treating this as a fresh collection requirement. Ken has a HELOC lined up and a planned roof replacement — the bundle framing (roof + solar together) is what converted him on this call and should be the closer's opening frame.
